steps.title: Find the repeated idea
steps.content: The passage repeatedly emphasizes where Begay gets dye materials: plants and vegetables from Arizona, Arizona dock roots, and clay from nearby soil. Those examples all point to local resources shaping her wool-dyeing process.

steps.title: Avoid narrow or invented claims
steps.content: A focuses too narrowly on colors in one rug. C invents difficulty finding dock root. D adds acclaim and weaving techniques that the text never mentions. The broad main idea is local materials in dyeing. The answer is B.
